title.S: IDF Strikes on 8th Artesh Tactical Airbase, Esfahan — F-14 Storage and Air Defense Destroyed

location.S: 8th Artesh Air Force Tactical Airbase, north of Esfahan City, Esfahan Province, Iran

description.S: The IDF struck the 8th Artesh Air Force Tactical Airbase north of Esfahan City on March 7. Satellite imagery from March 8 shows approximately ten impact points including extensive cratering on the base's taxiway. The IDF announced it struck structures storing F-14 fighter jets and air defense systems at the base. The base hosts F-14s, F-7s, and PC-7 aircraft. These strikes extend US-Israeli air dominance into central Iran.

title.S: Strike on 4th Artesh Ground Forces Aviation Base, Esfahan City

location.S: 4th Artesh Ground Forces Aviation Base, Esfahan City, Esfahan Province, Iran

description.S: The combined force struck the 4th Artesh Ground Forces Aviation Base in Esfahan City on March 7. Satellite imagery captured March 8 shows damage to several structures north of the base's runway, including several large hangars or warehouses. The base hosts rotary-wing aircraft.

title.S: Runway and Taxiway Cratering at 3rd Artesh Tactical Airbase, Kabudarahang, Hamedan Province

location.S: 3rd Artesh Air Force Tactical Airbase, Kabudarahang, Hamedan Province, Iran

description.S: Commercially available satellite imagery confirmed the combined force cratered runways and taxiways at the 3rd Artesh Air Force Tactical Airbase in Kabudarahang, Hamedan Province, likely rendering it operationally impaired.

title.S: Satellite BDA: Shiraz South Missile Base — Ground-Penetrating Strikes on Underground Facilities

location.S: Shiraz South Missile Base, south of Shiraz, Fars Province, Iran

description.S: Satellite imagery from March 7 shows the combined force used ground-penetrating munitions to target underground facilities at the Shiraz South Missile Base. The base is reportedly one of at least 25 Iranian missile bases capable of launching medium-range ballistic missiles. Israeli strikes during the June 2025 war caused only light above-ground damage; the March 2026 strikes appear to target hardened underground infrastructure.

title.S: Satellite BDA: Khojir Military Complex — Additional Structures Destroyed (March 7 Imagery)

location.S: Khojir Military Complex, Hajarabad, Tehran Province, Iran

description.S: Satellite imagery from March 7 confirmed additional strikes destroyed several structures flanked by earthen berms at the Khojir Military Complex, beyond the mixing and casting buildings identified on March 6. A James Martin Center analyst assessed the March 6 strikes had hit mixing and casting buildings; the March 7 imagery shows further destruction at this key solid-fuel missile production facility.

title.S: Satellite BDA: Shahroud Military Complex — Mixing/Casting Buildings Struck (March 7 Imagery)

location.S: Shahroud Military Complex, Semnan Province, Iran

description.S: A James Martin Center analyst assessed on March 7 that the combined force struck mixing and casting buildings at the Shahroud Military Complex in Semnan Province. Satellite imagery from March 7 confirmed this damage. This complements the earlier IDF announcement (ID 213) that the Shahroud facility was struck within the past week; the March 7 imagery provides specific building-type confirmation.

title.S: Satellite BDA: Chamran Missile Base, Jam, Bushehr Province — Above-Ground Structures Damaged

location.S: Chamran Missile Base, Jam, Bushehr Province, Iran

description.S: Satellite imagery from March 6 showed that the combined force damaged above-ground structures at the Chamran Missile Base in Jam, Bushehr Province. Iran stores Qiam-1 ballistic missiles at this base, which have a range of approximately 800 kilometers. A prior strike on IRGC missile bases in Jam was recorded on February 28 (ID 7); this BDA confirms damage to the specifically named Chamran facility.

title.S: Satellite BDA: Shiraz West Missile Base — Above-Ground and Underground Structures Struck (Twice)

location.S: Shiraz West Missile Base, west of Shiraz, Fars Province, Iran

description.S: Satellite imagery from March 7 confirmed strikes on above-ground and underground structures at the Shiraz West Missile Base, west of Shiraz, Fars Province. The combined force has struck this base at least twice during the current conflict.

title.S: Satellite BDA: Khorgu Missile Base, North of Bandar Abbas, Hormozgan Province

location.S: Khorgu Missile Base, north of Bandar Abbas, Hormozgan Province, Iran

description.S: Satellite imagery from March 7 showed damage to several structures at the Khorgu Missile Base north of Bandar Abbas. It was unclear whether the damage reflects recent strikes or earlier combined force strikes on the base around March 1.

title.S: IDF Strikes: IRGC Ground Forces Compound + Internal Security C2 + Basij Base + 50 LEC Ammo Bunkers, Tehran

location.S: Tehran City and Tehran Province, Iran

description.S: The IDF announced on March 8 that the IAF struck an IRGC Ground Forces compound, an unspecified 'internal security command center,' an unspecified Basij base, and 50 Law Enforcement Command ammunition bunkers in Tehran. OSINT accounts additionally confirmed and geolocated combined force airstrikes on a Basij base near Yas Boulevard in southeastern Tehran City.

title.S: Strikes on IRGC HQ + LEC Police Station, Khorramabad; LEC HQ Sites in Borujerd, Kuhdasht, Eslamabad-e Gharb, Abdanan

location.S: Khorramabad (Lorestan); Borujerd (Lorestan); Kuhdasht (Lorestan); Eslamabad-e Gharb (Kermanshah); Abdanan (Ilam), Iran

description.S: The combined force struck multiple internal security sites in western Iran on March 8. Confirmed targets include an IRGC headquarters and an LEC police station in Khorramabad, and LEC headquarters in Borujerd, Kuhdasht (both Lorestan Province), Eslamabad-e Gharb (Kermanshah Province), and Abdanan (Ilam Province). CTP-ISW noted protests occurred in Eslamabad-e Gharb, Borujerd, and Abdanan during the December 2025–January 2026 protest wave.

title.S: Strike on Artesh Ground Forces 92nd Division, Ahvaz, Khuzestan Province

location.S: Artesh Ground Forces 92nd Division, Ahvaz, Khuzestan Province, Iran

description.S: Anti-regime media reported that the combined force struck the Artesh Ground Forces 92nd Division in Ahvaz, Khuzestan Province on March 8. The Artesh has historically deployed its ground forces to support suppression of protests in previous protest movements.

title.S: Strike on LEC Headquarters, Shahin Shahr, Esfahan Province

location.S: Shahin Shahr, Esfahan Province, Iran

description.S: OSINT accounts reported combined force airstrikes on an LEC headquarters in Shahin Shahr, Esfahan Province on March 8, part of the ongoing campaign against Iran's internal security infrastructure.

title.S: Strike on Fuel Tank, Qeshm International Airport, Qeshm Island

location.S: Qeshm International Airport, Qeshm Island, Hormozgan Province, Iran

description.S: OSINT analysts reported the combined force struck a fuel tank at Qeshm International Airport on Qeshm Island in the Persian Gulf on March 8. Qeshm Island has significant IRGC military infrastructure and is strategically located at the entrance to the Strait of Hormuz.

title.S: IDF Kills Brig. Gen. Abu al Qassem Babaeiyan — Supreme Leader Office Military Chief and Khatam ol Anbia CoS

location.S: Iran (location unspecified)

description.S: The IDF confirmed on March 8 that it killed Brigadier General Abu al Qassem Babaeiyan on March 7. Babaeiyan had been appointed as Supreme Leader Office Military Chief after the combined force killed his predecessor, Mohammad Shirazi, on February 28. He had also been appointed Khatam ol Anbia Central Headquarters Chief of Staff after the IDF killed Brigadier General Ali Shadmani in the June 2025 Israel-Iran War. Babaeiyan's killing represents the second cycle of decapitation-and-replacement targeting of both these positions within a single campaign.

title.S: Iran 8 Missile Barrages at Israel — Cluster Munition Wounds 6 in Central Israel (March 7–8)

location.S: Central Israel

description.S: Iran launched eight missile barrages targeting Israel between 3:00 PM ET on March 7 and 3:00 PM ET on March 8. Magen David Adom reported that an Iranian cluster munition wounded at least six individuals in central Israel. Iran has now launched at least six missile barrages at Israel per day since March 2.

title.S: Iranian Drone Damages Desalination Plant, Bahrain; Drones Intercepted at Shaybah Oil Field, Saudi Arabia

location.S: Bahrain and Shaybah, Saudi Arabia

description.S: An Iranian drone damaged a desalination plant in Bahrain during the March 7–8 period; Bahrain's water and electricity authority stated the country's water supply was not affected. Separately, Saudi Arabia intercepted two Iranian drones targeting the Shaybah Oil Field in Saudi Arabia.

title.S: Iranian Projectile Kills 2, Wounds 12 in Residential Area, Kharj, Saudi Arabia — IRGC Claims Radar Targeting

location.S: Kharj (Al Kharj), Saudi Arabia

description.S: The Saudi Civil Defense Authority reported on March 8 that an unspecified projectile fell on a residential area in Kharj (Al Kharj), Saudi Arabia, killing two individuals and wounding 12 others. The IRGC separately announced it had targeted radar systems in Kharj — Prince Sultan Airbase is located in Al Kharj, suggesting the residential impact may have been caused by an errant missile aimed at the airbase.

title.S: IDF 100+ Strikes in Lebanon Past 24 Hours — Radwan Force HQ + Training Compound (March 7–8)

location.S: Southern Lebanon and Bekaa Valley, Lebanon

description.S: The IDF conducted over 100 strikes in Lebanon in the 24 hours ending March 8, and a total of 600 strikes using 820 munitions in Lebanon since February 28. Targets on March 7–8 included weapons depots, dozens of military sites, a Radwan Force headquarters, a Radwan Force training compound, rocket launchers, and fighters. The IDF also issued another evacuation warning for all residents south of the Litani River.

title.S: IDF Kills 5 IRGC Quds Force Officers in Beirut (Hassani, Beyaz, Rasouli, Ahmadlou, Ali)

location.S: Beirut, Lebanon

description.S: The IDF confirmed killing five IRGC Quds Force officers in Beirut on March 7–8: Majid Hassani (Quds Force commander responsible for transferring funds to Hezbollah and procuring weapons), Ali Reza Beyaz (Lebanon Corps intelligence branch commander), Ahmad Rasouli (Palestine Corps intelligence officer collecting intel for Palestinian militias), Hossein Ahmadlou (intelligence operative on Israel), and Abu Mohammad Ali (Hezbollah's representative in the Palestine Corps coordinating between Hezbollah and the Corps).

title.S: Hezbollah Kills 2 IDF Soldiers Attacking Armored Bulldozer, Southern Lebanon (First IDF KIA Since Fall 2024)

location.S: Southern Lebanon

description.S: The IDF reported that Hezbollah attacked an Israeli D9 armored bulldozer in southern Lebanon, killing two IDF soldiers. This is the first time Hezbollah has killed IDF soldiers since the Fall 2024 Israel-Hezbollah conflict. The IDF also reported Hezbollah has been launching most attacks from positions deep inside southern Lebanon, suggesting use of medium- or long-range systems.

title.S: Hezbollah Attack on IDF Rehavam Camp, Ramla (~135km from Lebanon Border)

location.S: Rehavam Camp, Ramla, central Israel

description.S: Hezbollah claimed attacking the IDF Rehavam Camp in Ramla, approximately 135 kilometers from the Israel-Lebanon border. The IDF reported Hezbollah has been firing from positions deep inside southern Lebanon, indicating the use of medium- or long-range weapons such as the Khaibar-1 rocket, Fadi 6 rocket, or Fateh-110 ballistic missile. This represents a significant escalation in Hezbollah's operational reach into central Israel.

title.S: Militia Drone Hits Radar at Harir Airbase / Erbil International Airport (Kataib Sarkhat al Quds, March 7–8)

location.S: Harir Airbase / Erbil International Airport, Erbil Province, Iraq

actor.S: Kataib Sarkhat al Quds (Harakat Hezbollah al Nujaba front)

description.S: Iranian-backed Iraqi militia Kataib Sarkhat al Quds claimed a drone attack targeting Harir Airbase at the Erbil International Airport on March 7. An unspecified Iraqi source told Iranian state media on March 8 that a drone hit a radar system at the base. Kataib Sarkhat al Quds announced on March 5 that it operates under Harakat Hezbollah al Nujaba's direction.

title.S: Strikes on Garmdareh Missile Base, Alborz Province — Solid-Fuel Chemical Plant and Underground Structures

description.S: The IDF struck a chemical plant in Garmdareh, Alborz Province west of Tehran on March 3 that the IDF stated produced raw materials for solid-fuel surface-to-surface missiles. Satellite imagery from March 6 confirmed this facility is the Garmdareh Missile Base, with strikes damaging both above-ground and underground structures. The James Martin Center satellite imagery from March 4 showed 8+ buildings damaged. The IDF previously destroyed a related missile integration facility at Garmdareh during the June 2025 12-Day War.
